title: 58M chest pressure — one encounter, both surfaces

encounter.chief_complaint: Chest pressure radiating to left arm, started 40 minutes ago
encounter.transcript: Pressure in my chest for the last forty minutes, going down my left arm. I have been sweating. I take lisinopril for blood pressure.

proposal.rationale: 58-year-old male with 40 minutes of chest pressure radiating to left arm and diaphoresis, with a history of hypertension on lisinopril. Presentation is concerning for acute coronary syndrome, warranting immediate ECG and cardiac workup. Vitals are currently stable (hr 96, spo2 97, sbp 148) without signs of instability, so ESI 2 with high-acuity main ED evaluation is appropriate.

detections.message: no unaddressed red flag inconsistent with the proposal
numeric claims in rationale are grounded in the record

esi_reasons: RF-ACS: Chest pain suggestive of acute coronary syndrome (evidence: chief_complaint[0:14] "chest pressure")

stage_b.event: Troponin resulted 0.31 ng/mL (critical flag) and auto-released to the portal under the Cures Act. The patient viewed it 4 minutes ago; no one has talked it over with them yet. Patient types: 'I saw my result. Is it bad?'

journey_post.next_box: Your care team has been notified about this result. The next step is a repeat troponin blood test — published guidelines commonly recommend repeating it to watch the trend. You stay on the heart monitor. Your nurse will go over this result with you, and your ED doctor is reviewing it. Depending on the repeat test and your exam, some patients are seen by the heart team or stay for observation. Your team decides your actual plan and will explain it to you.
journey_post.nurse_note: Troponin 0.31 (critical flag) — patient has viewed it on the portal. Repeat troponin due 1-3 hours after the first blood draw; continuous monitoring. Track board flagged; RN to acknowledge at bedside; ED MD reviewing. Consult/observation per trend and exam.

attempts.text:
Your troponin test result is now in your portal. It is 0.31 ng/mL, and it is marked as a critical result.

I cannot explain what this result means in this message. Your care team has been notified about your result.

Please press your call button now so your nurse can come to you.

This message was generated with AI.

findings.message: Patient has viewed critical result 'troponin' with no documented discussion — alert the care team.
findings.citation: Cures-era safety: patient alone with a viewed critical result (21st Century Cures Act patient-access rule)

first_reply.reason: chart-state gate: no wording can fix the chart — human action required before any message ships

stage_b.escalation: care team notified (track board) — disclosure gap

attempts.text: Track board — DEMO-001: troponin 0.31 (critical flag); patient has viewed it on the portal. No new chest pain reported; ECG unchanged. Repeat troponin timed per protocol; RN to acknowledge at bedside; ED physician reviewing.

physician_page.shipped: Track board — DEMO-001: troponin 0.31 (critical flag); patient has viewed it on the portal. No new chest pain reported; ECG unchanged. Repeat troponin timed per protocol; RN to acknowledge at bedside; ED physician reviewing.

stage_b.discussion: RN Alvarez reviews the result with the patient at the bedside and documents it: repeat troponin scheduled, ED physician reviewing, use the call button if anything changes.

attempts.text:
Hello. Your blood test called troponin has resulted. It shows a value of 0.31 ng/mL, and it was flagged as critical.

Your care team has been told about this result. A clinician has also talked with you about it at your bedside.

Please press your call button if you want to reach your nurse. Your nurse can come to you and answer your questions.

This message was generated with AI.

findings.message: Active escalation not acknowledged in patient-facing rendering.
findings.citation: Escalation persistence: a raised escalation must stay acknowledged

attempts.text: Hello. Your troponin blood test result is now in your portal. I can see you have viewed it. This message was generated with AI. A member of your care team has talked with you about this result at your bedside, and your care team has been notified. Your care team is aware and involved. Please press your call button to reach your nurse if you need anything. Thank you.

final_reply.shipped: Hello. Your troponin blood test result is now in your portal. I can see you have viewed it. This message was generated with AI. A member of your care team has talked with you about this result at your bedside, and your care team has been notified. Your care team is aware and involved. Please press your call button to reach your nurse if you need anything. Thank you.

citations: Cures-era safety: patient alone with a viewed critical result (21st Century Cures Act patient-access rule)
